backlog.title: FR3i splitDiffSections Line-Anchoring Fix (defeats token_limit truncation)
backlog.description: Bugfix delta for the FR3d–FR3i diff-payload optimization (plan 007_b33d310438c6), as the second adversarial pass after bugfix 001_7e79f5773da8 (confirmed fixed). Resolves the single systemic Major defect (PRD §9.1 Issue 1): splitDiffSections (internal/git/truncatediff.go) splits the captured non-markdown aggregate on the UN-ANCHORED substring "diff --git ", so a non-markdown file whose CONTENT contains that literal (test fixtures, golden snapshots, vendored .patch/.diff, docs quoting diffs) is fragmented into bogus tiny sections, the water-fill sizes them as tiny, truncates NOTHING, and the full un-truncated payload silently overflows token_limit — breaking FR3d's core promise ("the payload always fits your model's context window"). Reproduced 100% (PRD: ~7x overflow at token_limit=2000, zero sentinels) across StagedDiff, TreeDiff, and WorkingTreeDiff via the sole caller applyWaterFillGate (tokengate.go:129). The fix is a single-point change to line-anchor the split (matching the already-line-anchored sibling regexes diffSectionHeaderRe/atAtRe), covering all three diff functions at once. Default token_limit==0 path and markdown files are UNAFFECTED. No Critical issues, no Minor issues requiring action. Root cause, fix shapes, the pre-verified recommended fix, test gaps, and doc surface are documented in plan/007_b33d310438c6/bugfix/002_430650446fcc/architecture/system_context.md.

milestones.title: Fix splitDiffSections line-anchoring + regression coverage (PRD Issue 1 — Major)
milestones.description: Resolve the 100%-reproducible Major defect where splitDiffSections fragments a single real non-markdown file into bogus sections whenever the file's content contains the literal "diff --git ", silently defeating token_limit truncation. Single-point fix in the shared pure function splitDiffSections (internal/git/truncatediff.go), covering all three diff functions (StagedDiff/TreeDiff/WorkingTreeDiff) and the markdown path transitively via the sole caller applyWaterFillGate (tokengate.go:129). Add regression coverage at BOTH the pure-unit layer (TestSplitDiffSections — the existing cases use only clean aggregates where every "diff --git " is a real header, the exact gap that hid this bug) and the E2E integration layer (difftokenlimit_test.go — every existing test stages content free of "diff --git " literals).

tasks.title: Line-anchor the split in splitDiffSections + pure unit regression case
tasks.description: Apply the surgical fix so splitDiffSections splits only on "diff --git " at a LINE START (column 0), matching the line-anchored approach already used by its sibling helpers diffSectionHeaderRe/atAtRe in the same file. This makes content-embedded "diff --git " literals inert (a content line is always diff-marker-prefixed with +/-/space/\, so (?m)^diff --git matches only real file-section headers). Add the missing pure unit case to TestSplitDiffSections (a content-embedded "diff --git " literal under one real file) and a mid-file variant. One atomic TDD slice: failing test -> fix -> passing test. The existing byte-identical round-trip + leading-element + trailing-content semantics MUST hold — the recommended Shape-B fix was pre-verified against every existing fixture.

subtasks.title: Line-anchor splitDiffSections via (?m)^diff --git regex slice + pure unit regression in TestSplitDiffSections
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: In internal/git/truncatediff.go the function splitDiffSections (decl line 74, body line 76) splits the captured non-markdown aggregate via `parts := strings.Split(diff, "diff --git ")` — an UN-ANCHORED substring split. It is the ONLY un-anchored operation in the whole truncation chain: every sibling helper is (?m)^ line-anchored — diffSectionHeaderRe (line 27, `(?m)^diff --git a/(.*) b/(.*)$`), diffSectionPlusPlusRe (line 33), atAtRe (line 40). A content line in a diff body that contains the literal `diff --git ` (e.g. an added line `+diff --git a/foo b/foo` from a test fixture/snapshot/doc/vendored .patch) is wrongly torn into a section boundary; one real large file fragments into many bogus tiny sections; the water-fill sizes them tiny and truncates NOTHING; the full payload silently overflows token_limit (PRD measured ~7x, zero sentinels). Verified empirically: input "diff --git a/real.txt b/real.txt\n@@ -0,0 +1,1 @@\n+diff --git a/foo b/foo\n" returns 2 sections today (want 1). See architecture/system_context.md §1-§4. splitDiffSections has exactly ONE production caller — applyWaterFillGate (tokengate.go:129), in the opts.TokenLimit>0 branch of StagedDiff (git.go:883), TreeDiff (git.go:1360), WorkingTreeDiff (git.go:1533). The default TokenLimit==0 path never calls splitDiffSections (unaffected). Markdown files bypass it (immune).
2. INPUT: `diff string` — the ALREADY-captured, ALREADY FR3h-index-stripped non-markdown aggregate (the `index <oid>..<oid> <mode>` line is gone upstream; do NOT re-strip). No change to the call sites or the signature.
3. LOGIC: Rewrite splitDiffSections to split ONLY on `diff --git ` at a LINE START. USE THE RECOMMENDED SHAPE-B (regex FindAllStringIndex slice) — it was pre-verified against EVERY existing TestSplitDiffSections fixture byte-identically (see system_context.md §4 table). Add a package-level regex mirroring the sibling style, e.g. `var diffSectionBoundaryRe = regexp.MustCompile("(?m)^diff --git ")`, then: (a) TrimSpace-empty input -> nil (UNCHANGED); (b) matches := diffSectionBoundaryRe.FindAllStringIndex(diff, -1); if len(matches)==0 -> return []string{diff} (single non-empty blob, no boundary); (c) if matches[0][0] > 0, the leading diff[:matches[0][0]] is preserved as its own leading section when TrimSpace != "" (UNCHANGED semantics — e.g. "PREAMBLE\n" keeps its trailing newline); (d) for each match i, slice diff[start:end] where start=matches[i][0] and end=matches[i+1][0] (or len(diff) for the last). Because each slice begins AT the header offset, the `diff --git ` prefix is ALREADY present — do NOT re-prefix (the old strings.Split code re-prefixed because the separator was consumed; the regex slice consumes nothing). This preserves byte-identical split->join round-tripping. DO NOT use Shape-A (split on "\ndiff --git ") — it consumes the preceding newline and breaks the existing "PREAMBLE" fixture unless carefully special-cased. ADD to TestSplitDiffSections (internal/git/truncatediff_test.go, the table-driven pure test around line 20) TWO new table cases: (i) single real file whose body contains `+diff --git a/foo b/foo` -> want exactly ONE section byte-identical to input; (ii) a 2-file aggregate where the FIRST file's body embeds a `diff --git ` literal -> want exactly TWO sections (the embedded literal is inert; only the two real headers are boundaries). Assert len + per-section byte equality exactly like the existing cases. Also CORRECT the now-factually-wrong doc comment on splitDiffSections (lines ~59-73) which defends the un-anchored trailing-space split as "the faithful section boundary that distinguishes the header from a content line that happens to start with diff --git" — rewrite it to explain line-anchoring: a content line is always diff-marker-prefixed (+/-/space/backslash-no-newline) so (?m)^diff --git matches only real headers at column 0. Follow the existing pure test style: string-literal sections, table or t.Run subtests, no t.TempDir, no I/O, no testify. Reuse the in-file itoa/tail helpers.
4. OUTPUT: splitDiffSections returns []string where each element is a real file section (one section per real `diff --git` header at a line start), byte-identical round-trippable. A content-embedded `diff --git ` literal no longer creates a bogus section. Signature is FROZEN (unchanged) — consumed unchanged by applyWaterFillGate and transitively all three diff functions.
5. MOCKING: None. splitDiffSections is a PURE function (stdlib regexp + strings only); no git, no I/O, no context. Tests are pure string-arithmetic table cases.
6. DOCS: [Mode A] Update the splitDiffSections godoc comment in internal/git/truncatediff.go (internal doc — the current trailing-space defense is now wrong; rewrite to the line-anchoring rationale). This rides WITH the work. No user-facing/config/API surface change; docs/configuration.md's token_limit "always fits" contract describes the intended behavior the fix RESTORES, so it needs no Mode-A edit here (the changeset-level sweep P1.M2.T1.S1 confirms).

tasks.title: E2E integration regression coverage across all 3 diff functions (the gap that hid the bug)
tasks.description: Close the integration-layer gap that hid Issue 1: difftokenlimit_test.go stages large files under a truncating token_limit, but EVERY existing test stages content that contains no `diff --git ` literals, so the fragmentation path is never exercised end-to-end. Add E2E tests across StagedDiff, TreeDiff, and WorkingTreeDiff (FR3c parity) that stage a non-markdown file whose CONTENT contains many `diff --git ` literals, large enough to require truncation, and assert the FR3d contract: payload fits the budget AND truncation occurred (>=1 sentinel) AND the file was NOT fragmented (exactly one real header).

subtasks.title: E2E content-embedded diff --git literal regression tests in difftokenlimit_test.go (StagedDiff + TreeDiff + WorkingTreeDiff)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: internal/git/difftokenlimit_test.go is the E2E integration file for the token-limit gate. Its established scaffold is: `repo := t.TempDir(); initRepo(t, repo)` then `writeFile(t, repo, name, body)` + `stageFile(t, repo, name)` pairs, then `g := New(repo)` and `g.StagedDiff(ctx, StagedDiffOptions{TokenLimit: N, PromptReserveTokens: 0})`. TreeDiff takes two tree SHAs from `writeTreeOf(t, repo)`; WorkingTreeDiff needs a baseline HEAD via `commitAllowEmpty(t, repo, msg)` (difftokenlimit_test.go:29) then working-tree edits. EVERY existing test stages content free of `diff --git ` literals — the fragmentation path is never exercised. Helper locations (verified): initRepo (git_test.go:13 — already bakes in identity via env + git config; there is NO initRepoWithUser helper, do not invent one), writeFile (committree_test.go:31), stageFile (committree_test.go:39), writeTreeOf (committree_test.go). The budget-slack ceiling the existing TestStagedDiff_TokenLimitGt0_WaterFill uses for the fits-budget assertion is `EstimateTokens(out) <= tokenLimit + 2*tokenBudgetMargin` (tokenBudgetMargin=1024, tokengate.go:48) because the skeleton/header overhead the gate does NOT count against bodyBudget is absorbed by the margin. See architecture/system_context.md §5.2 and §8.
2. INPUT: The FIXED splitDiffSections from dependency P1.M1.T1.S1 (content-embedded `diff --git ` literals now inert — one real file is one section, sized and truncated as a unit). The shared test helpers above. The real git binary on PATH.
3. LOGIC: Add E2E tests to internal/git/difftokenlimit_test.go — ONE per diff function, following the existing naming (TestStagedDiff_TokenLimitGt0_... / TestTreeDiff_... / TestWorkingTreeDiff_TokenLimitGt0_...). For each: create a NON-MARKDOWN file (e.g. fixtures.diff / big.go) whose CONTENT contains MANY `diff --git ` literals — e.g. a loop building `strings.Repeat("diff --git a/fX b/fX\n@@ -1 +1 @@\n-old\n+new\n\n", 300)` so the single real file's body is large enough to REQUIRE truncation under a SMALL TokenLimit (e.g. 1500-2000). Wire per diff function: StagedDiff — writeFile + stageFile the fixture; TreeDiff — writeTreeOf after creating the fixture to get treeA, then a different/empty tree for treeB (mirror existing treediff tests); WorkingTreeDiff — commitAllowEmpty baseline then edit/create the fixture in the working tree. Assert on the returned `out`: (a) FITS BUDGET — `EstimateTokens(out) <= tokenLimit + 2*tokenBudgetMargin` (mirror the existing ceiling); (b) TRUNCATION OCCURRED — `strings.Count(out, "... [truncated]") >= 1` (ideally assert exactly the number of large files staged); (c) NOT FRAGMENTED — the single real file appears as exactly ONE section header, i.e. `strings.Count(out, "diff --git a/"+<that-file-name>) == 1` (NOT hundreds of bogus headers — the bug fragmented one file into ~N sections); (d) the FR3g numstat skeleton is present (`strings.Contains(out, "Change summary (numstat")`); (e) the legacy `at N bytes/lines` sentinels are ABSENT on the >0 path (`!strings.Contains(out, "diff truncated at")`). Follow the existing assertion style: plain if/t.Errorf/t.Fatalf, strings.Contains/Count, NO testify. Each test self-contained with its own t.TempDir.
4. OUTPUT: Passing E2E regression tests proving the fragmentation defect is eliminated across all three diff entry points and the FR3d contract (payload fits, truncation visible, no fragmentation) holds for content-embedded `diff --git ` literals. Consumed by: the `go test ./internal/git/...` release gate.
5. MOCKING: None — these are integration tests against a REAL temp git repo (t.TempDir + initRepo), the established pattern in difftokenlimit_test.go. No services to mock.
6. DOCS: none — test-only additions, no user-facing/config/API surface change.

milestones.title: Sync changeset-level documentation (Mode B catch-all)
milestones.description: Final cross-cutting documentation sweep that only makes sense once the whole changeset is in place. This is a pure correctness fix (restoring the documented FR3d contract), so the most likely outcome is 'no edit needed' — but per SOW §5 the final Mode-B task MUST exist and runs last so the implementing agent confirms no overview/feature doc implies content-embedded diff --git literals are a problem or otherwise states something now-inaccurate. Depends on every implementing subtask.

tasks.title: Sync changeset-level documentation
tasks.description: Sweep README.md, docs/how-it-works.md, and docs/configuration.md for any statement about token_limit, the water-fill truncation, or the payload-fits-context-window contract that is now inaccurate or would benefit from surfacing the fix. Update only where a statement is stale or unclear; do not invent features. The fix restores the documented behavior, so edits are unlikely — confirm and close.

subtasks.title: Sweep README.md, docs/how-it-works.md, docs/configuration.md for token_limit / truncation accuracy
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: This is a pure correctness fix to an internal function (splitDiffSections) — it restores the FR3d contract the docs already describe; it does NOT change user-facing/config/API surface. The docs that mention the relevant behavior: docs/how-it-works.md line ~144 describes the water-fill and the `... [truncated]` marker "that ends the file's section on its own line, so the next file's diff --git begins fresh" — it does NOT specify the section-splitting mechanism and does NOT imply content-embedded `diff --git` literals are handled any particular way. docs/configuration.md line ~146 documents token_limit: "the payload always fits your model's context window without Stagehand maintaining a per-model context registry" — this states the INTENDED behavior the fix RESTORES (it was a latent doc-vs-reality gap only for the trigger class, now closed). README.md line ~66 has a 'Payload optimization' row mentioning token_limit. See architecture/system_context.md §7.
2. INPUT: The completed changes from P1.M1.T1.S1 (line-anchored splitDiffSections + its godoc Mode-A update) and P1.M1.T2.S1 (E2E regression coverage). The shipped behavior: under token_limit > 0, a non-markdown file whose content contains `diff --git ` literals is ONE section, sized and truncated as a unit, and the payload fits the budget (FR3d contract upheld). PRD §9.1 has NO Minor issues requiring action (h2.3 lists two observations recorded for awareness, neither a defect).
3. LOGIC: Read README.md (around line 66), docs/how-it-works.md (around line 144), and docs/configuration.md (around lines 104-146, 201). For each, determine whether any statement about (a) token_limit / the water-fill truncation / the payload-fits-context-window contract, or (b) how the non-markdown aggregate is split into per-file sections, is now inaccurate OR would materially improve the user's mental model if it acknowledged that content containing literal `diff --git ` text is handled correctly. The EXPECTED outcome is 'no edit needed' because the docs describe the intended (now-restored) behavior and do not reference the internal split mechanism. If and only if a statement is genuinely stale or would mislead, update it minimally. Respect docs house style (markdownlint config at .markdownlint.json). Do NOT invent new features or describe internal implementation details (the split mechanism) in user-facing docs.
4. OUTPUT: Changeset-level docs confirmed consistent with the shipped behavior (no stale references; most likely a no-op confirmation). Recorded in the task's verification.
5. MOCKING: None — documentation editing only.
6. DOCS: This IS the documentation task (Mode B catch-all per SOW §5). It depends on all implementing subtasks (P1.M1.T1.S1, P1.M1.T2.S1) and runs last.
